Government_Defense_Tech.description: Companies selling software/services to government agencies (PLTR, BAH, LDOS, SAIC)

contract_backlog_ratio.description: Secured long-term backlog as % of annual revenue
contract_backlog_ratio.signal: Higher = stronger moat, more predictable revenue

contract_renewal_rate.description: % of contracts renewed with same agency on re-compete
contract_renewal_rate.signal: Higher = high switching costs, agency locked in

gov_revenue_concentration.description: Government revenue as % of total revenue
gov_revenue_concentration.signal: 20-40% is optimal stability. >60% = dependency risk. <10% = not a real gov contractor

agency_diversification.description: Number of distinct agency customers / revenue spread

contract_type_mix.description: Ratio of cost-plus vs fixed-price vs T&M contracts
contract_type_mix.signal: Cost-plus = lower risk, fixed-price = higher margin but more risk

clearance_moat.description: % of workforce with active security clearances
clearance_moat.signal: High clearance workforce = massive barrier to entry for competitors

set_aside_eligibility.description: Qualifies for small business set-aside contracts
set_aside_eligibility.signal: Temporary high-value moat but expires with growth

compression_triggers.budget_sequestration: Federal budget cuts directly reduce addressable market
compression_triggers.recompete_loss: Losing a major recompete signals moat erosion
compression_triggers.clearance_bottleneck: If clearance processing slows, growth stalls
compression_triggers.administration_change: New president may shift priorities away from tech vendors
compression_triggers.audit_findings: DCAA audit issues can freeze contract awards

Enterprise_SaaS.description: B2B software sold on subscription (CRM, WDAY, HUBS, NOW)

net_revenue_retention.description: NRR - revenue from existing customers year over year including expansion

avg_contract_length.signal: Multi-year = switching cost moat. Month-to-month = no lock-in

platform_ecosystem_size.description: Number of third-party apps/integrations on the platform
platform_ecosystem_size.signal: Large ecosystem = network effect moat (Salesforce AppExchange)

data_gravity.description: Does the product accumulate proprietary customer data that's hard to migrate?
data_gravity.signal: CRM data, HR records, financial history = high switching cost

enterprise_customer_pct.description: % of revenue from enterprise (>1000 employees) vs SMB
enterprise_customer_pct.signal: Enterprise = sticky multi-year deals. SMB = high churn

magic_quadrant_position.signal: Leader = procurement shortlist default. Niche = at risk

compression_triggers.nrr_decline: NRR dropping below 100% = customers actively leaving
compression_triggers.platform_commoditization: Core feature gets built into larger platform (e.g., Slack into Teams)

Consumer_Social.description: B2C social/content platforms (META, SNAP, PINS, RDDT)

daily_active_users.signal: High DAU/MAU = habitual use = strong network effect

time_spent_per_user.signal: More time = more ad inventory = more revenue per user

arpu_trend.description: Average Revenue Per User trend (growing or shrinking)
arpu_trend.signal: Growing ARPU = monetization improving. Shrinking = losing ad share

content_creator_economy.description: Number of creators earning money on the platform
content_creator_economy.signal: Creator economy = supply-side network effect (YouTube, TikTok)

social_graph_depth.description: How interconnected are users (friends, followers, groups)
social_graph_depth.signal: Deep graph = massive switching cost (leaving means losing connections)

compression_triggers.ad_revenue_shift: Advertisers moving budget to competitor platform
compression_triggers.feature_clone: Competitor clones core differentiator (IG Stories killed Snap)
compression_triggers.regulatory_action: Privacy regulation limits ad targeting capability

content_exclusivity.description: % of content that is proprietary vs commoditized
content_exclusivity.signal: Proprietary content = moat. Public domain / AI-generated = no moat

credential_value.description: Does the platform grant recognized credentials/degrees?
credential_value.signal: Accredited degrees = strong moat. Certificates = weak moat

ai_replaceability.description: Can an AI chatbot provide the same service?
ai_replaceability.signal: If ChatGPT can do what you do, you have no moat

compression_triggers.ai_tutoring_parity: AI reaches feature parity with human tutoring
compression_triggers.free_alternative: Khan Academy, YouTube, or AI provides same content free
compression_triggers.credential_devaluation: Employers stop valuing the credentials offered

take_rate.description: Platform commission as % of transaction value
take_rate.signal: High take rate only sustainable with strong network effects

supply_side_lock_in.description: How hard is it for freelancers to leave (reputation, reviews)
supply_side_lock_in.signal: Portable reputation = weak moat. Platform-locked reputation = strong

buyer_repeat_rate.description: % of buyers who return for repeat purchases
buyer_repeat_rate.signal: High repeat = marketplace working. Low = one-time transactions

ai_automation_exposure.description: % of listed services that AI can now perform
ai_automation_exposure.signal: Writing, design, coding tasks increasingly automated

headcount_trend.signal: Declining headcount = AI replacing human agents

average_handle_time.signal: If AHT is decreasing via AI, fewer humans needed

client_concentration.signal: High concentration = one client leaving is catastrophic

automation_adoption_rate.signal: Industry rapidly automating, margin pressure accelerating

contract_stickiness.description: Average contract length and switching cost
contract_stickiness.signal: Multi-year contracts buy time but don't prevent eventual compression
